title: The Chautauquan, Vol. 05, July 1885, No. 10
author: Chautauqua Literary and Scientific Circle; Chautauqua Institution; Flood, Theodore L., 1842- [Editor]

quoteFirst: It comprised all that was required of the servant, from eight in the morning, exactly at which hour Phileas Fogg rose, till half-past eleven, when he left the house for the Reform Club—all the details of service, the tea and toast at
quoteLast: , the shaving-water at thirty-seven minutes past nine, and the toilet at twenty minutes before ten.
title: Around the World in Eighty Days
author: Verne, Jules, 1828-1905; Towle, George M. (George Makepeace), 1841-1893 [Translator]

quoteFirst: “Well, y’ronor, I was on m’post on Simpson street las’ night an’ at
quoteLast: , I hear a commotion in front of the Idle Hour Movie Theater, at 1833 Simpson Street, Saul Bloch, proprietor. I seen the prisoner here bein’ thrown outa the theater by some men. They was kickin’ and punchin’ him. A woman was screamin’ ‘He kissed me! He kissed me!’ I ast her did she want to make a complaint against him and she said yes, she did. So I arrested him.”
title: Apes and Angels
author: Connell, Richard Edward, 1893-1949

quoteLast: , the shaving-water at thirty-seven minutes past nine, and his attendance at his master's toilet at twenty minutes to ten, and so on.
title: Round the World in Eighty Days
